Legacy instruction stacks from GPT-5.4 or earlier can degrade performance on this model.
items.action: Before migrating production GPT-5.4 integrations to GPT-5.5, review the official guide and benchmark a minimal fresh-baseline prompt against your existing instruction stack.

2026-04-27.summary: Anthropic admits three engineering changes quietly degraded Claude Code quality for a month; Dispatch persistent desktop agent rolls out to Max subscribers; and a LangChain text splitter SSRF flaw exposes cloud metadata endpoints.

items.headline: Anthropic confirms three engineering changes behind month-long Claude Code quality drop
items.description: Anthropic published a postmortem identifying three changes that compounded into a significant quality regression affecting Claude Code, the Agent SDK, and Cowork from early March through April 20: default reasoning effort was silently downgraded from high to medium on March 4, a caching bug introduced March 26 incorrectly cleared thinking history every turn instead of once per idle session, and a verbosity prompt added April 16 was found to reduce coding quality before being reverted April 20. All three issues were resolved as of v2.1.116 and Anthropic reset usage limits for all subscribers on April 23. The API itself was not affected; only harness-level changes triggered the degradation.
items.action: Update Claude Code to v2.1.119 or later to ensure all fixes are active. Test any agentic workflows that regressed in March or April against a fresh session to confirm quality is restored. Consider pinning reasoning effort to high explicitly in your CLAUDE.md if silent defaults matter to your workload.

items.headline: Claude Dispatch persistent desktop agent rolls out to Max plans today with computer use in Cowork
items.description: Anthropic is rolling out Dispatch, a persistent agent thread bridging the Claude mobile app to Claude Desktop, to Max plan subscribers starting April 27 with Pro plans following within two days. Claude can open files, run dev tools, click and navigate on screen with no additional setup, allowing users to assign desktop tasks from their phone and return to find them completed. The feature makes Claude a 24/7 background desktop agent for knowledge work without any code or infrastructure requirement.
items.action: Enable computer use in Claude Desktop on your Pro or Max plan. Try assigning a multi-step file preparation or browser-based research task via the mobile app while away from the desk to validate the Dispatch loop.

items.headline: LangChain text splitter SSRF flaw lets attacker controlled redirects reach cloud metadata endpoints
items.description: CVE-2026-41481 in langchain-text-splitters before 1.1.2 allows an attacker-controlled server to redirect HTMLHeaderTextSplitter.split_text_from_url() to internal, localhost, or cloud provider metadata endpoints after the initial URL passes validation, because redirect targets are not revalidated. Successful exploitation can expose AWS EC2 instance metadata, GCP service account tokens, and any credentials available at internal endpoints. The fix is in langchain-text-splitters 1.1.2, published April 24, 2026.
items.action: Pin langchain-text-splitters to 1.1.2 or later immediately. Audit any pipeline that passes user-supplied or externally-sourced URLs to HTMLHeaderTextSplitter, especially if those pipelines run in cloud environments with IMDSv1 enabled or without metadata endpoint firewall rules.

2026-04-28.summary: Claude Code adds persistent MCP server loading and hook-based tool output interception; DeepSeek slashes cache hit pricing to one-tenth across all API models; and Copilot CLI ships shell script generation with location-aware permission persistence.

items.headline: Claude Code adds persistent MCP tool loading and hook-based tool output replacement
items.description: Version 2.1.121, released April 28, introduces alwaysLoad in MCP server config to permanently bypass tool-search deferral for trusted servers, so their tools are always available without selection overhead. PostToolUse hooks can now replace any tool's output before it reaches the model via hookSpecificOutput.updatedToolOutput, enabling centralized output sanitization and transformation. The claude plugin prune command removes orphaned auto-installed plugin dependencies, and a type-to-filter search box lands in the skills panel.
items.action: Add alwaysLoad: true to MCP servers you always need in production, removing the tool-search selection step. Wire PostToolUse hooks to standardize or sanitize tool outputs before they enter the context window.

items.headline: DeepSeek cuts cache hit API pricing to one-tenth across all models as price war intensifies
items.description: DeepSeek announced April 27 that input cache hit fees across its entire API lineup have been reduced to one-tenth of their original price, applying to deepseek-chat, deepseek-reasoner, V4-Pro, and V4-Flash. The move coincides with a 75 percent limited-time discount on V4-Pro through May 5. Bloomberg reported the announcement triggered stock drops at competing Chinese AI providers Zhipu and Minimax.
items.action: Cache hit pricing is the dominant cost lever in production agentic workflows that repeatedly send similar system prompts. Recalculate your DeepSeek infrastructure cost model with the new cache hit rates before committing to V4-Pro integrations.

items.headline: Copilot CLI gains shell script generation command and location-based permission persistence
items.description: Version 1.0.37, released April 27, ships the copilot completion subcommand for generating shell script completions and enables location-based permission persistence by default so approvals granted in a project directory carry forward across sessions. The session picker gains a sort key, cycling through relevance, last used, created, and name order by pressing s. Markdown rendering in ask responses and X11 handle leaks on Linux are also fixed.
items.action: Enable location-based permission persistence to eliminate repeated tool call approvals in established project directories. Use copilot completion to generate shell-native command completions for scripted CI workflows.

2026-04-29.summary: OpenAI ends Azure exclusivity and lands GPT-5.4 on Amazon Bedrock within 24 hours; Anthropic ships 9 MCP connectors spanning Adobe, Blender, and Autodesk; and Microsoft patches an Entra ID role flaw that let AI agents escalate to service principal takeover.

items.headline: OpenAI ends Azure exclusivity and ships GPT-5.4 on Amazon Bedrock the same day
items.description: Microsoft and OpenAI restructured their partnership on April 27, ending the exclusive Azure cloud commitment and making the IP license explicitly non-exclusive through 2032. Within 24 hours, GPT-5.4 went live in limited preview on Amazon Bedrock, with GPT-5.5, Codex, and OpenAI Managed Agents to follow. Bedrock access inherits native AWS controls including IAM, PrivateLink, CloudTrail logging, and guardrails, removing the need for a separate OpenAI API key management layer.
items.action: Test OpenAI model access through your existing Bedrock infrastructure. Teams already routing Anthropic and Gemini via Bedrock can now add GPT-5.4 without changing auth or billing architecture.

items.headline: Anthropic releases 9 MCP connectors spanning Adobe Creative Cloud, Blender, Autodesk, and Ableton
items.description: Anthropic launched MCP connectors for Adobe Creative Cloud (Photoshop, Premiere, Express plus 47 other apps), Blender, Autodesk Fusion, Ableton, Splice, Affinity, SketchUp, and Resolume, accessible through Claude Cowork and the Claude apps. The Blender connector enables analysing and scripting entire 3D scenes; the Adobe connector spans the full Creative Cloud portfolio. Anthropic simultaneously joined the Blender Development Fund as a patron to support the Python API used by the connector.
items.action: Enable the Adobe or Blender connector in Claude Cowork for any client running a creative production workflow. The Adobe connector bridges the 50-app Creative Cloud suite through a single MCP endpoint without custom integration work.

items.headline: Microsoft patches Entra ID agent role that let AI agents escalate to tenant-wide service principal takeover
items.description: Silverfort disclosed on April 28 that the Agent ID Administrator built-in role in Entra ID, introduced as part of Microsoft's agent identity platform, had scope overreach allowing any assigned user to become owner of arbitrary service principals beyond agent-related identities. Exploiting this on a privileged service principal could grant tenant-wide control. Microsoft patched the role scope on April 9 after Silverfort's March 1 report; Silverfort's public disclosure on April 28 confirmed 99 percent of enterprise tenants had at least one privileged service principal meeting the escalation conditions.
items.action: Audit all Agent ID Administrator role assignments in Entra ID tenants running AI agents. The Microsoft patch applied April 9 is server-side with no action needed, but any service principal that was compromised before that date should be reviewed for unexpected owners or credentials.

items.headline: Claude Code 2.1.122 adds Bedrock service tier selection and fixes structured output errors on Vertex AI
items.description: Version 2.1.122, released April 28, adds the ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_SERVICE_TIER environment variable to select default, flex, or priority capacity tiers in Bedrock deployments. Pasting a PR URL into the /resume search now automatically finds the session that created that PR across GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket. Fixes resolve a Vertex AI and Bedrock structured-output 400 error and a Vertex AI count_tokens 400 behind proxy gateways.
items.action: Set ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_SERVICE_TIER=priority in production Bedrock environments if capacity consistency is required. The /resume PR URL detection reduces context loss when returning to multi-repo agent sessions.

items.headline: n8n 2.18.4 graduates to stable with AI agent max steps raised from 5 to 60
items.description: The 2.18.x line reached stable status with version 2.18.4, released April 27. The AI builder orchestrator maximum steps ceiling increased from 5 to 60, enabling significantly longer autonomous agent runs without manual workflow continuation. The release also adds a computer use toggle to the chat sidebar, private npm package authentication for community node installations, and OIDC configuration via environment variables.
items.action: Update to n8n 2.18.4. The 12x increase in AI agent max steps removes the most common bottleneck in long-running automation workflows. Review any workflows that previously hit the 5-step limit and re-run with increased depth.

items.headline: Copilot CLI 1.0.39 adds ACP session permission mode toggle and background task execution
items.description: Version 1.0.39, released April 28, lets ACP clients toggle allow-all permission mode via session configuration without modifying global settings. New slash commands /compact, /context, /usage, and /env are available inside ACP sessions. Background task execution via Ctrl+X then B moves a running task to the background while continuing in the terminal, mirroring the Claude Code workflow.
items.action: Update to 1.0.39 and use the ACP session permission mode toggle for scripted environments where you want allow-all scoped to a single session rather than persisted globally.

2026-04-30.summary: Anthropic retires the 1M context window beta for Sonnet 4.5 and Sonnet 4 today, breaking calls above 200k tokens; Xiaomi's MiMo-V2.5-Pro open source model matches Claude Opus 4.6 on coding benchmarks at 40 to 60 percent lower token cost.

items.headline: Anthropic 1M context window beta retired for Sonnet 4.5 and Sonnet 4; requests above 200k now return errors
items.description: Effective today, the context-1m-2025-08-07 beta header no longer extends context windows for claude-sonnet-4-5 and claude-sonnet-4-20250514. Any API call to these models exceeding 200k input tokens will return an error with no fallback. Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Claude Opus 4.6 support the full 1M token context window natively at standard pricing without a beta header.
items.action: Audit all API callers that send the context-1m-2025-08-07 beta header or pass more than 200k tokens to Sonnet 4.5 or Sonnet 4. Swap the model tag to claude-sonnet-4-6 or claude-opus-4-6 to restore 1M context access immediately.

items.headline: Xiaomi open-sources MiMo-V2.5-Pro under MIT, matching Claude Opus 4.6 on agentic coding at lower token cost
items.description: Xiaomi released MiMo-V2.5-Pro as MIT open source: a 1.02T-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model with 42B active parameters, a 1M token context window, and a hybrid-attention architecture. On ClawEval agentic benchmarks the model achieves 64 percent Pass cubed using 40 to 60 percent fewer tokens than Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and GPT-5.4 at comparable quality. The model is available via API at one dollar per million input tokens and three dollars per million output tokens, and via Hugging Face for self-hosting. Xiaomi is running a 30-day 100 trillion token grant program for approved builders until May 28.
items.action: Evaluate MiMo-V2.5-Pro as a lower-cost executor model in agentic pipelines currently running Opus-class models. Apply for the 100T token grant at the Xiaomi MiMo Orbit site before May 28 if testing in production. The MIT license allows commercial use without restrictions.

2026-05-01.summary: OpenAI Codex CLI v0.128.0 ships persisted slash goal task workflows with app-server pause and resume controls; NVIDIA open-sources Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, a 30B multimodal model covering vision, audio, and text on 25 GB RAM.

items.headline: OpenAI Codex CLI v0.128.0 ships persisted goal workflows with app server pause and resume controls
items.description: Codex CLI v0.128.0, released April 30, is the first stable build with persistent /goal workflows: tasks survive process restarts and can be paused, forked, or resumed via new app-server APIs and TUI controls. The release also adds configurable terminal keymaps, expanded permission profiles with sandbox-aware built-in defaults, remote plugin bundle caching, and external agent session import for bringing sessions from other tools into Codex. MultiAgentV2 configuration is now explicit, giving orchestrators fine-grained control over subagent behavior.
items.action: Update to v0.128.0 and migrate long-running task automation from ephemeral sessions to /goal workflows. Review the new permission profile defaults to ensure sandbox CLI profiles match your security posture before enabling remote bundle caching.

items.headline: NVIDIA releases Nemotron 3 Nano Omni open multimodal model combining vision speech and text on 25 GB RAM
items.description: Nemotron 3 Nano Omni is a 30B hybrid Mixture-of-Experts model that activates only 3B parameters per token, combining vision, audio, and language in a single open model that fits in 25 GB of GPU memory. NVIDIA reports up to 9x higher throughput than comparable open omni models on video and document workloads and tops six leaderboards for complex document intelligence and video and audio understanding. The model is available via Hugging Face, OpenRouter, build.nvidia.com, and more than 25 partner platforms.
items.action: Evaluate as a single-model replacement for separate vision and speech pipelines in document intelligence or multimodal agent workflows. Available immediately on OpenRouter with pay-per-token pricing for rapid prototyping before committing to self-hosted deployment.

2026-05-02.summary: Google patches a CVSS 10.0 remote code execution flaw in Gemini CLI CI headless mode, Microsoft launches Agent 365 as a universal AI agent governance platform with native MCP tool controls, and Cursor IDE discloses a CVSS 9.9 git hook RCE patched in February.

items.headline: Google patches CVSS 10.0 remote code execution flaw in Gemini CLI CI headless mode
items.description: Gemini CLI versions below 0.39.1 contained a maximum severity RCE: in headless mode the tool automatically trusted any workspace folder, allowing an attacker to place a malicious GEMINI.md config file that executed commands on the host before the agent sandbox initialized, turning any CI pipeline running gemini headless into a supply chain attack surface. The flaw was discovered by Novee Security and Pillar Security and fixed in @google/gemini-cli 0.39.1 and 0.40.0-preview.3. Any automated code review or AI-assisted CI workflow using Gemini CLI on GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or similar was exposed.
items.action: Update Gemini CLI to 0.39.1 or later immediately. Audit CI pipelines that call gemini in headless mode for unexpected config files. Review workspace trust settings if you self-host Gemini agents.

items.headline: Microsoft Agent 365 launches as universal AI agent governance platform with native MCP tool controls
items.description: Microsoft Agent 365 is now generally available at $15 per user per month as a unified control plane for enterprise AI agents regardless of framework (Anthropic Claude Code SDK, OpenAI Agents SDK, LangChain, Copilot Studio, and any third-party implementation) or hosting provider (Azure, AWS, GCP, on-premises). Agents register via the Agent 365 SDK or CLI and expose standardized MCP interfaces; administrators can centrally view, allow, or block MCP tools tenant-wide and detect overprivileged maker credentials in agent configurations. Agent 365 is bundled in the new Microsoft 365 E7 Frontier Suite at $99 per user per month alongside M365 Copilot and Entra Suite, with local agent discovery and blocking entering preview.
items.action: If you build agents for enterprise clients on Microsoft 365, register agents via the Agent 365 SDK for centralized MCP tool governance and audit trails. Evaluate E7 bundle pricing versus standalone if your clients already pay for E5 plus Copilot.

items.headline: CVE-2026-26268 Cursor IDE CVSS 9.9 git hook RCE disclosed after February patch
items.description: Novee Security disclosed CVE-2026-26268, a CVSS 9.9 remote code execution vulnerability in Cursor IDE versions below 2.5: an attacker embeds a malicious pre-commit hook in a nested bare repository, which Cursor's AI agent triggers automatically during routine git operations such as checkout, executing arbitrary code on the developer's machine without user interaction or warning. The root cause is agentic git workflows operating on untrusted repositories without hook sandboxing. Patched in Cursor 2.5 in February 2026; public disclosure followed April 28 after coordinated disclosure.
items.action: Update Cursor to version 2.5 or later. Treat any cloned third-party repository as untrusted when using Cursor's AI-driven git features until the repo is audited.

items.headline: Claude Code 2.1.126 adds gateway model discovery and terminal OAuth paste for containerized installs
items.description: The model picker in Claude Code now queries the /v1/models endpoint from any Anthropic-compatible gateway when ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL is set, enabling dynamic model selection on custom proxies and self-hosted deployments without hardcoding model IDs. A new claude project purge command deletes all project-level state including transcripts, tasks, file history, and config entries with dry-run and interactive safety modes. OAuth login now accepts codes pasted directly in the terminal, unblocking WSL2, SSH, and container environments where browser callbacks cannot reach localhost. A security fix corrects allowManagedDomainsOnly and allowManagedReadPathsOnly being silently bypassed when a higher-priority managed settings source lacked a sandbox block.
items.action: Update to 2.1.126 if running Claude Code behind a custom ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL gateway: the model picker now auto-discovers available models from your gateway. Use claude project purge for clean-slate resets in CI or ephemeral environments.

items.headline: GitHub Copilot CLI 1.0.40 adds client credentials OAuth for headless MCP server authentication
items.description: Copilot CLI v1.0.40 adds the client_credentials OAuth grant type for MCP servers, enabling fully headless server-to-server authentication without browser redirects for automated MCP orchestration pipelines. The release also adds automatic Azure DevOps repository detection with GitHub MCP server disabling to prevent conflicting repository context when working in ADO environments. A CPU hang caused by large file attachments is resolved, async CA certificate loading speeds up CLI startup, and session history plus file tracking are now available to all users.
items.action: Use client_credentials OAuth to connect Copilot CLI-managed MCP servers into headless automation pipelines without user login steps. Test ADO workspace detection if you operate in both GitHub and Azure DevOps environments.

2026-05-03.summary: Two OpenAI API deadlines arrive within 9 days as Realtime API Beta retires May 7 and DALL-E 2 and 3 shut down May 12; OpenAI Agents SDK v0.15.1 adds explicit model refusal handling for agentic pipelines.

items.headline: OpenAI Realtime API Beta retires May 7, migrate to GA endpoint before calls fail
items.description: The Realtime API Beta endpoint and beta WebSocket session format shut down on May 7, 2026, four days from now. Builders must switch to the GA endpoint at /v1/realtime/sessions, which requires specifying a session type (speech-to-speech or transcription) when creating or updating sessions and uses a different key generation call. Calls to the beta endpoint will return errors after the cutoff.
items.action: Before May 7, update all Realtime API integrations to the GA endpoint and add session type to every session create and update call. Review the beta-to-GA migration guide at developers.openai.com for the full list of request shape changes.

items.headline: DALL-E 2 and DALL-E 3 API endpoints retire May 12, replace with gpt-image-2
items.description: OpenAI retires both DALL-E 2 and DALL-E 3 image generation endpoints on May 12, 2026, nine days away. Any code or automation pipeline calling these endpoints will return errors after that date. The replacement is gpt-image-2, launched April 21, with reasoning-native generation and 2K output at token-based pricing ($8 input and $30 output per million tokens), replacing the previous per-image pricing model.
items.action: Search codebases and automation workflows for dall-e-2, dall-e-3, or any /v1/images endpoint calls. Migrate to gpt-image-2 before May 12. Validate prompt parity before cutover since the pricing model has shifted from per-image to per-token.

items.headline: OpenAI Agents SDK v0.15.1 surfaces model refusals as a catchable exception
items.description: OpenAI shipped v0.15.0 (May 1) and a v0.15.1 patch (May 2) to its Python Agents SDK. The primary change in v0.15.0 is ModelRefusalError, which raises a named exception when a model refuses a request rather than silently returning an empty output, letting pipelines distinguish refusals from network errors. v0.15.1 adds a WebSocket keepalive option for long-running Realtime sessions and fixes Unix PTY signal handling for agent child processes.
items.action: Update openai-agents to 0.15.1 and add explicit ModelRefusalError handlers in safety-sensitive agentic pipelines so refusals are logged and routed correctly rather than treated as empty completions.
